Built with Quarto · Hosted on GitHub Pages

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text: Quarto makes it straightforward to publish a polished organisation website directly from a GitHub repository. This post walks through the setup used for this very site.

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text: Quarto makes it straightforward to publish a polished organisation website directly from a GitHub repository. This post walks through the setup used for this very site.

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Prerequisites

A GitHub account / organisation
Quarto CLI installed locally
Basic familiarity with Markdown

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Step 1 — Create the Repository
On GitHub, create a repository named <org>.github.io (for organisations) or <username>.github.io (for personal sites). This special naming convention tells GitHub Pages to serve the repo at https://<org>.github.io.
For example:
Organisation: julesg-org
Repository:   julesg-org.github.io   ← served at https://julesg-org.github.io

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Step 2 — Initialise a Quarto Website
# In your cloned repository folder:
quarto create project website .
This creates the minimal _quarto.yml and index.qmd scaffold.

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Step 3 — Customise _quarto.yml
Open _quarto.yml and set at minimum:
project:
  type: website

website:
  title: "Your Org Name"
  site-url: https://your-org.github.io
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: "Home"
        href: index.qmd
      - text: "About"
        href: about.qmd

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Step 4 — Add GitHub Actions
Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml to automate builds on push:
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: quarto-dev/quarto-actions/setup@v2
      - run: quarto render
      - uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./_site

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Step 5 — Enable GitHub Pages
In your repository: Settings → Pages → Source → gh-pages branch.
Push to main and the site deploys automatically! 🎉

title: Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation
text:
Next Steps

Add blog posts under blog/posts/<post-name>/index.qmd
Add project pages under projects/<project-name>/index.qmd
Customise colours in styles.css

title: About Jules G Org
text:
Jules G Org is an open-source organisation focused on data science, software engineering, and making technical knowledge more accessible.
We publish reproducible analyses, reusable tooling, and detailed write-ups on everything from exploratory data analysis to deployment pipelines.

title: About Jules G Org
text:
Jules G Org is an open-source organisation focused on data science, software engineering, and making technical knowledge more accessible.
We publish reproducible analyses, reusable tooling, and detailed write-ups on everything from exploratory data analysis to deployment pipelines.

title: About Jules G Org
text:
What We Do

📊 Data Science — reproducible notebooks, statistical analyses, and visualisation libraries.
🛠️ Open-Source Tools — small, focused packages and utilities available on GitHub.
✍️ Technical Writing — in-depth blog posts and tutorials that walk through real problems step by step.

title: About Jules G Org
text:
How This Site Works
This site is built with Quarto and deployed automatically to GitHub Pages via a GitHub Actions workflow every time changes are pushed to the main branch.
Each project in the julesg-org GitHub organisation can contribute its own page here by following the pattern demonstrated in projects/sample-project.

title: About Jules G Org
text:
Get Involved
We welcome contributions! Open an issue or pull request on any of our repositories, or reach out via the links above.

title: Sample Project

title: Sample Project
text:
About This Project
Sample Project is a demonstration repository that shows how any repository in the julesg-org GitHub organisation can contribute a page to the main organisation website at julesg-org.github.io.
This page was generated from content inside the main website repository (julesg-org.github.org). In a real workflow, the content on this page would mirror or embed information from the sample-project repository.

title: Sample Project
text:
Features

✅ Clean, reproducible structure (Quarto + Markdown)
✅ Links back to the Jules G Org home page
✅ Integrated into the main site’s navigation and search
✅ Automatic dark / light mode support
✅ Category tags for filtering on the Projects listing

title: Sample Project
text:
Getting Started
# Clone the sample project repository
git clone https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project.git
cd sample-project

# Install dependencies (example for a Python project)
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run the project
python main.py

title: Sample Project
text:
Project Structure
sample-project/
├── README.md          # Overview and quick-start guide
├── main.py            # Entry point
├── src/               # Source code
│   └── analysis.py
├── data/              # Input data (not committed — see .gitignore)
├── notebooks/         # Jupyter / Quarto notebooks
└── tests/             # Unit tests

title: Sample Project
text:
Usage Example
# Example: loading and analysing data with this project
from src.analysis import load_data, summarise

df = load_data("data/sample.csv")
summary = summarise(df)
print(summary)

title: Sample Project
text:
Results & Outputs
This section would normally contain visualisations or key findings. For a real project, embed Quarto code cells here so that outputs are reproduced automatically every time the site is built:
```{python}
# Uncomment the lines below and add real code for live output
# import pandas as pd
# df = pd.read_csv("data/sample.csv")
# df.describe()
```

title: Sample Project
text:
Links



Resource
URL




Source code
github.com/julesg-org/sample-project


Issues
GitHub Issues


Main site
julesg-org.github.io










How to add your own project




Create a new folder in projects/ inside the main repo.
Copy this file (projects/sample-project/index.qmd) as a starting template.
Fill in your project’s title, description, and content.
Open a pull request — once merged, your project page appears automatically.





Part of Jules G Org · Built with Quarto

title: Welcome to Jules G Org
text: Welcome to the Jules G Org blog! This is the first post on our new Quarto- powered website, so let’s use it to explain what this place is all about.

title: Welcome to Jules G Org
text: Welcome to the Jules G Org blog! This is the first post on our new Quarto- powered website, so let’s use it to explain what this place is all about.

title: Welcome to Jules G Org
text:
What Is Jules G Org?
Jules G Org is a GitHub organisation that collects open-source projects spanning data science, tooling, and technical writing.

title: Welcome to Jules G Org
text:
How This Site Is Built
The site is generated using Quarto — an open-source scientific and technical publishing system that can render .qmd files (Markdown + optional code cells) into polished HTML websites, PDFs, and more.






flowchart LR
    A[Edit .qmd files] --> B[git push to main]
    B --> C[GitHub Actions]
    C --> D[quarto render]
    D --> E[gh-pages branch]
    E --> F[julesg-org.github.io]




Figure 1: How the site is built and deployed






Editing Content
All content lives in .qmd files at the root of the julesg-org.github.org repository:



Path
What it controls




_quarto.yml
Site-wide config (navbar, theme, footer)


index.qmd
Home / landing page


about.qmd
About page


blog/posts/<post>/index.qmd
Individual blog posts


projects/sample-project/index.qmd
Sub-project page


styles.css
Custom CSS overrides

title: Welcome to Jules G Org
text:
What’s Next?
Look out for posts on:

🐍 Python and R data science workflows
📦 Building reusable packages
🔧 DevOps and CI/CD for data projects

Stay tuned, and feel free to open an issue or pull request on GitHub!
